title: chlorogalum pomeridianum subsp. pomeridianum
intro: Chlorogalum pomeridianum subsp. pomeridianum, also known as the evening glory lily or wild hyacinth, is a species of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Chlorogalum pomeridianum subsp. pomeridianum include treating fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a natural remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used externally or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
